title: Fermented Uruguayan Butternut Squash Empanadas
description: A deliciously crumbly and fermented twist on traditional Uruguayan empanadas, featuring roasted butternut squash and a tangy mother sauce base.

ingredients.name: mother sauce
ingredients.notes: fermented mixture of onion, garlic, and bell pepper

instructions: Roast the butternut squash in the oven for 45 minutes, or until tender.
Let the squash cool, then mash it in a large bowl.
In a separate bowl, mix together the mother sauce, salt, and sesame oil.
On a lightly floured surface, roll out the empanada dough to a thickness of 1/4 inch.
Cut out circles of dough using a cookie cutter or the rim of a glass.
Place a tablespoon of mashed squash on one half of each dough circle, leaving a 1/2 inch border around the edges.
Brush the edges with sesame oil or vegan butter.
Fold the dough in half to form a half-moon shape, pressing the edges to seal.
Brush the tops of the empanadas with sesame oil or vegan butter.
Bake the empanadas in the preheated oven for 20-25 minutes, or until golden brown.
